title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: VARIOUS PASSAGES IN THE GOSPELS Make sure you focus on the verses for each day, as these important events are taken from several readings of scripture across several chapters. These are chosen to highlight the beginning of the life of John the Baptist and to help identify the sovereign circumstances and themes God works out in John's life.
weekTheme: Boys Assume, Men Notice God has a purpose for you. PREPARING YOU FOR YOUR PLACE When you study the lives of great men in history you will learn something interesting. Their family dynamics, childhood experiences, and the community they grew up in had a massive impact on them. Sometimes, they grew up with gratitude and blessing and sought to live up to their full potential. Other times, they worked hard to overcome massive problems and dysfunctions, and the struggle made them strong. God knows how to prepare a man for his calling. God knows the family he needs to be born in, the mentors who need to surround him, and the situations that will shape him. God will put someone at the right time and place to form him into the man he needs. That means you need to pay attention to the things happening around you right now because they are clues to what you may be called to yourself. God will use the things you go through to help you become the person he wants you to be. But many people miss it and don’t pay attention. So they don’t learn the lessons they need and have to wait longer than necessary because they aren’t ready. This week, we are going to see how God used John's environment and circumstances to produce a prophet for the people. It will give you some clues to how God works and what he may be doing around you.

days.title: Filled With The Spirit
days.scripture: LUKE 1:8-17 hen Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God, he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense. And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside. Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense. When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear. But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to call him John. He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth, for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born. He will bring back many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God. And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the parents to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
days.reflection: It can be so amazing and weird when you see a woman who is pregnant. It’s just crazy to think that a whole human being is growing inside of her. It’s even crazier to realize that at one point you were growing inside your mom like that. We rarely think about the time that we were in our moms’ wombs; it’s almost like it didn’t happen. No one remembers being born or the first few months of their life. But that season actually really impacts us. Scientists tell us that what a mother eats, how happy or sad she is, and what happens around her can have a direct effect on the baby inside her. She communicates a ton of her experiences through chemical signals sent through the placenta to the baby. Babies in the womb know way more about what’s going on around them than we can imagine. The process of birth and growth is just incredible. Babies can end up having psychological problems, learning challenges, and emotional struggles later in life all based on what happened when they were inside their mom when she was pregnant. They can also be happier, less stressed, and more resilient based on how their moms were feeling when they were pregnant. Today’s reading tells us that John the Baptist was filled with the Holy Spirit even when he was inside his mom’s womb. Elizabeth was filled with wild joy at the news that she was going to have a son. John was loved by his parents long before he was born, and he would have been so wanted and welcomed into his home. The angel said that he was going to be “a joy and delight to them.” This would have a huge impact on his sense of worth and value when he grew up, but his parents’ love wasn’t the most important factor of his birth. He was filled with the Holy Spirit when he was a baby. In the Bible, the Holy Spirit is sent to empower men to serve God and accomplish his will. It's God in us, enabling us to please and serve him with a power beyond ourselves.John was under God's direct empowerment and guidance from his earliest days. This doesn’t mean he would have been a perfect kid, but it means he would have been a godly one. He would have been set apart for a special purpose and given a vision of a divine sense of destiny. And this influence, from his earliest days, would have given him a strong sense of calling and confidence as he grew up into a man. It's important as you grow into manhood that you are filled with the Holy Spirit. You are not going to be able to do all God wants or reach your full potential without God giving you power, help, wisdom, and guidance. If you just drift along with your friends, you will live an aimless, frustrated, and passive life. But the Holy Spirit can move inside you to give you guidance as to what decisions to make. He gives you power to overcome temptation and stand up to the crowd. He gives you a sense of calling and a purpose that pulls you through the hard times you face in these challenging teenage years. All great men in redemptive history have had a definitive encounter with the Holy Spirit. The Spirit rested on Jesus at his baptism, empowered Peter to return after his denial, shook the believers in the book of Acts, and the Holy Spirit will help you too. If you are going to take your place in the story of God, you are going to need the help of the Holy Spirit. He will guide you into everything God has for you. You are not left on your own to figure it out.
questions: Can you think of your first or one of your first memories as a kid? What was it? Was it good or bad?

A lot of Christians talk about the Holy Spirit, but have you ever experienced the presence of the Holy Spirit? Maybe in a church service? Or in your conscience? What was the experience like if you had one?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share the first encounter they had with the power of the Holy Spirit. What role does the Holy Spirit play in their life today?

thoughts.verse: “You fathers—if your children ask for a fish, do you give them a snake instead? Or if they ask for an egg, do you give them a scorpion? Of course not! So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to fill you with the Holy Spirit. Ask him in faith and take a moment to wait on him. If there is something beating you down or you are really struggling with, ask him to help empower you today.

days.title: Prepared As A Priest
days.scripture: JOHN 1:8-17 Once when Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God, he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense. And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside. Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense. When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear. But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to call him John. He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth, for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born. He will bring back many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God. And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the parents to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
days.reflection: We are all shaped and impacted by our Dads. Your Dad was impacted by his Dad. You will be impacted by your dad. Your kids will be impacted by you when you are a dad. Fathers play a massive role in shaping our vision of life. They shape our views on money, sex, marriage, work, and play. But one of the most significant ways they influence us is in our view of God. They shape how we see the character of God, the role of the Bible, how we deal with challenges in life. They teach us what it looks like to have integrity. Dads have a massive impact on their families. If a family member comes to Christ, the influence they have on the rest of the family coming to Christ is disproportionality powerful if it’s a dad. Check this out. If it’s: A wife - 18 percent Children - 22 percent A father - 94 percent! That’s why it’s important to notice who God gave John the Baptist as a father, and the family he was born into. Zechariah, John’s dad, was a priest. He was a man of faith, dedicated to serving God and living with integrity. Verse 8 reads: Once when Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God… God positioned John in a home where he would be raised by a priest. He was born into the priestly tribe and would have seen what his Dad did when he served. He would have been raised in a home where the scriptures were central, worship practiced, and the details of priestly life explained. Just like all dads share with their families about what they do for a living, John would have been shaped in a way that stirred his heart for God and imagination about who he could be growing up. In fact, the angel told Zechariah, He is never to take wine or other fermented drink. This wasn’t because he was a Nazarite, but because he was called in the line of a priest. Leviticus 10:8-10 says, Then the LORD said to Aaron, “You and your descendants must never drink wine or any other alcoholic drink before going into the Tabernacle… You must distinguish between what is sacred and what is common, between what is ceremonially unclean and what is clean. John was to be set apart. He was to carry the calling of his Father. He was going to play a massive role as a leader and prophet for God's people. He was going to need to be consecrated. Have you ever considered why you are in the family you are in? Have you ever thought about why your dad is taking you through the Primal Path? It’s because he is preparing you to do something incredible with your life. God has put you with a Dad who loves you and is committed to helping you take your place in the story of God. To help you fulfill your calling and destiny. To give you wisdom and insight into your future. I know it may be hard spending all this time, and you may even wish you had more freedom like your friends to do whatever you want, but you aren’t some ordinary kid. You may not see it now, but God will do more than you can think or imagine. Open your eyes, young man, and pay attention. You are here for a reason. Lean in.
questions: In what ways have you seen your friends’ dads shape how they see life? Can you see any ways your friends act based on who their parents are?
In what ways have you been shaped by your dad? Can you give a couple of examples?
Ask your dad two or three ways his father shaped his life in a positive way growing up. How did this help him grow into a man?

thoughts.verse: My child, listen when your father corrects you. Don’t neglect your mother’s instruction. What you learn from them will crown you with grace and be a chain of honor around your neck.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for your family. Thank him for a godly home, and an intentional father who is raising you into your potential and destiny.

days.title: The Way Of The Wilderness
days.scripture: LUKE 1:67-80 Then his father, Zechariah, was filled with the Holy Spirit and gave this prophecy: “Praise the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has visited and redeemed his people. He has sent us a mighty Savior from the royal line of his servant David, just as he promised through his holy prophets long ago. Now we will be saved from our enemies and from all who hate us. He has been merciful to our ancestors by remembering his sacred covenant— the covenant he swore with an oath to our ancestor Abraham. We have been rescued from our enemies so we can serve God without fear, in holiness and righteousness for as long as we live. “And you, my little son, will be called the prophet of the Most High, because you will prepare the way for the Lord. You will tell his people how to find salvation through forgiveness of their sins. Because of God’s tender mercy, the morning light from heaven is about to break upon us, to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, and to guide us to the path of peace.” John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
days.reflection: It's not just the family you grow up in that shapes you; it’s the physical environment too. If you grow up by the sea, you will probably learn to surf. If you grow up in the woods, you’ll probably learn to hike. If you grow up in the snow, you’ll probably learn to ski. If you grow up by a lake, you’ll probably learn to fish. The last verse of today’s passage says, John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel. John grew up in the wilderness, and it shaped him tremendously. In the wilderness, John learned to hear God and trust God. The wilderness wasn’t just a random place or a tiny detail in John's story. It was symbolic and strategic. It was a place of separation and preparation. Remember how the Israelites had to wander in the wilderness to get rid of their rebellious hearts? Remember how Jesus went into the wilderness to face testing and temptation from the devil? It wasn’t a place most people choose to live and yet John voluntarily went and lived there. Why? He was serious about his calling. Anyone who gets serious about anything will be careful about the environments they get in. They will avoid places of temptation and distraction and get around places that help them grow and develop. This is true for sports, academics, being a creative, or pursuing a passion. An athlete wants to be around guys on the team who will push him to get better. When studying for exams, you get rid of video games and friends who want to mess around. You prepare and focus for what’s ahead. God was preparing John the Baptist to take his place in the story of God by removing distractions and temptations. God was working on John's heart. And the wilderness worked. When John showed up, he captured the attention of his generation. He set himself apart for God, and the people could tell. For John, the wilderness was a place of consecration. He lived like very few because he was going to make an impact like very few. If you are going to take your place in the story of God, you are going to have to choose a version of the wilderness. You are going to have to choose your friends carefully so they don’t pull you away from God. You are going to have to avoid temptation that could sabotage your potential for God and avoid distractions that rob you of your full potential. Guys your age discipline themselves all the time. They practice in summer for the fall season. Get up early to train, stay after school to work out. So many people do this for temporary things, but so few do it for eternal things. Choose your version of the wilderness. Don’t be afraid to get passionate about what God has for you. Get into a church community. Get around other friends who will spur you on. Paul urged Timothy when he was a young man in 1 Timothy 4:12, Don’t let anyone think less of you because you are young. Be an example to all believers in what you say, in the way you live, in your love, your faith, and your purity. God wants to train you and prepare you. Choose your wilderness. It’s a place of destiny.
questions: What is one way you can see how the place you have grown up has shaped you? What is something people do where you live that they don’t do in other places?
Have you ever said no to something out of passion or discipline? Maybe playing on a sports team or studying for an exam? How did it feel to do this? What do you learn about yourself?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about any “wilderness seasons" or preparation they have been through. What did they learn? What did God show them?

thoughts.verse: Don’t you realize that in a race everyone runs, but only one person gets the prize? So run to win! All athletes are disciplined in their training. They do it to win a prize that will fade away, but we do it for an eternal prize. So I run with purpose in every step. I am not just shadowboxing. I discipline my body like an athlete, training it to do what it should. Otherwise, I fear that after preaching to others I myself might be disqualified.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a vision of his purpose for you that enables you to say no to temptation and trivial things.

days.title: Strong In Spirit
days.scripture: LUKE 1:67-80 Then his father, Zechariah, was filled with the Holy Spirit and gave this prophecy: “Praise the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has visited and redeemed his people. He has sent us a mighty Savior from the royal line of his servant David, just as he promised through his holy prophets long ago. Now we will be saved from our enemies and from all who hate us. He has been merciful to our ancestors by remembering his sacred covenant— the covenant he swore with an oath to our ancestor Abraham. We have been rescued from our enemies so we can serve God without fear, in holiness and righteousness for as long as we live. “And you, my little son, will be called the prophet of the Most High, because you will prepare the way for the Lord. You will tell his people how to find salvation through forgiveness of their sins. Because of God’s tender mercy, the morning light from heaven is about to break upon us, to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, and to guide us to the path of peace.” John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
days.reflection: No man wants to be weak. This is true for our bodies, our spirits, and our minds. God has called men to be strong. The Bible is full of calls for men to be strong. It’s a call to the very heart of who we are. Paul said to Timothy, “Timothy, my dear son, be strong through the grace that God gives you in Christ Jesus.” (2 Timothy 2:1) In Ephesians 6:10, Paul urged the believers to “Be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power.” Look what King David says to his son Solomon on his deathbed.“I am going where everyone on earth must someday go. Take courage and be a man.” (1 Kings 2:2) Men are meant to be strong. In today's reading, we see an interesting detail about what happened to John in the wilderness. It says, John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel. Strong in spirit. What a cool phrase. Our world often portrays godly people as weak. Weak-minded, scared of doing the wrong thing, afraid of the big bad world. But the opposite is true. Believers are strong. Strong enough to handle rejection. Strong enough to go against the pressure of the crowd. Strong enough to speak up when everyone else is silent. To become a follower of Jesus is to grow in strength. John needed this strength for what God has called him to. He needed strength to call the nation of Israel to repent. He needed strength to handle the criticism and opposition that came with being a prophet. He needed strength to resist the temptations other men had fallen into. He needed strength to die well when he was beheaded in a tragic plot. Real strength isn’t about getting jacked so other guys respect you or fear you. It's about getting spiritually strong so other guys want to follow you. God prepared John the Baptist to take his place in God's story. He lived such an incredible life that we are still talking about him today! You must understand this. God wants to help you grow. He wants to take away your fear, insecurity, and struggles and make you a man who can hold your head high. Get rid of any thoughts or fears that becoming a man of faith will make you weak. It's quite the opposite. You are in the process of becoming stronger than you know.
questions: Who is the strongest person you know? What makes them so?
How are spiritual strength and physical strength similar? How are they different?
What is one thing you could do this week to grow “strong in spirit?”
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share 3 things they do to keep strong in spirit.

thoughts.verse: Be on guard. Stand firm in the faith. Be courageous. Be strong.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to continue to help you grow. Ask him to strengthen your love for him and your desire to please him. Offer him any areas where you are feeling weak.

days.title: Becoming An Intentional Man
days.scripture: LUKE 1:67-80 Then his father, Zechariah, was filled with the Holy Spirit and gave this prophecy: “Praise the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has visited and redeemed his people. He has sent us a mighty Savior from the royal line of his servant David, just as he promised through his holy prophets long ago. Now we will be saved from our enemies and from all who hate us. He has been merciful to our ancestors by remembering his sacred covenant— the covenant he swore with an oath to our ancestor Abraham. We have been rescued from our enemies so we can serve God without fear, in holiness and righteousness for as long as we live. “And you, my little son, will be called the prophet of the Most High, because you will prepare the way for the Lord. You will tell his people how to find salvation through forgiveness of their sins. Because of God’s tender mercy, the morning light from heaven is about to break upon us, to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, and to guide us to the path of peace.” John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
days.reflection: Godly men aren’t formed by accident. You won’t wake up one day and become a wise and courageous man. You are going to have to be intentional about the kind of man you want to be. You are going to have to pay attention, make choices, and resolve to keep moving forward. You are going to need courage and conviction, and strength and support. Reflect on what you have noticed about John the Baptist’s process of development. There was nothing accidental about it. God chose him, called him, and put him in a family with the right circumstances to inspire him. God sent him to the desert to learn to resist temptation and hear the voice of God. God was intentional in his plan and care for John. And God is doing the same thing for you if you slow down long enough to pay attention and look. The alternative to an intentional life is living a passive life. You don’t want to be a passive man. A passive man just stumbles through life letting anything and everything happen to him. He has no vision, no goals, no sense of call. There is a lot of regret in living a passive life. The way to become an intentional man is to start being an intentional teenager. This will set you apart from those around you. A passive teenager hangs out with whoever talks to him. An intentional teenager chooses friends carefully. A passive teenager doesn’t care about priorities. An intentional teenager organizes around what’s important. A passive teenager does what the most popular kids think is cool. An intentional teenager discerns what God thinks is cool. A passive teenager never thinks of the future. An intentional teenager plans for the future God has for him. A passive teenager is careless. An intentional teenager is careful. You are well on your way to being an intentional man. You are making your way from the shifts from boyhood into manhood, learning from some of the greatest men who have ever lived, and leaning into what God has for you. Just as God shaped John the Baptist's circumstance to get him ready, he is doing this all around you. Keep focusing and moving forward. The rewards of an intentional life are so, so worth it.
questions: Which of your friends do you think is the most intentional in how they live? Why?
Why do you think so many guys your age struggle with being passive? What areas do you feel this struggle in your own life?
Ask your dad how he learned to become intentional. Ask him to share a story about how being passive hurt him and how intentionality rewarded him.

thoughts.verse: I don’t mean to say that I have already achieved these things or that I have already reached perfection. But I press on to possess that perfection for which Christ Jesus first possessed me. No, dear brothers and sisters, I have not achieved it, but I focus on this one thing: Forgetting the past and looking forward to what lies ahead, I press on to reach the end of the race and receive the heavenly prize for which God, through Christ Jesus, is calling us.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a heart to become more intentional in your thinking. Ask him to give you a holy hatred for passivity.

extras: WEEK 2 MANSCHOOL STRATEGY SESSION MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge ACTIVITY: STRATEGY SESSION One of the greatest gifts a father can give his son is a vision of living intentionally. There is massive power in being an intentional father, but even more in raising intentional sons. Before you know it, your son is going to graduate and leave your home. But what will he leave with? I have written about a “before he leaves home list” in the book The Intentional Father, and shared about it as well in the Primal Path video course that you were given access to as a part of the discipleship cohort. But now, it’s time to let your son in on it. Take an evening to set up a brainstorming session with your son about what he wants to do and see before he leaves home. Get some huge sticky notes and post them all over the wall with some headings like these. (https://tinyurl.com/bdrepxab) Where does he want to go? Cool trips to take. What does he want to learn? Skills to develop. Who does he want to meet? People to network with. Fun things he wants to do. List of activities to work into the calendar. Questions he wants answered. Things to learn and explore. Make sure there is good food and explain the vision clearly. Very few guys his age will think this far ahead, and it will show him the power of big-picture thinking. You may want to take a photo of him in the middle of all the post-its to mark the moment.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
